creator: pluknet@gmail.com
text:
2009/1/26 Robert Watson <rwatson@freebsd.org>:
>
> On Sun, 3 Aug 2008, timur@FreeBSD.org wrote:
>
>> Robert, can you please take a look onto this PR? Logically, and comparing
>> with Linux implementation, changes to ACLs shouldn't affect mtime, as file
>> data doesn't change, only inode info(hence ctime is affected).
>
> Hi Timur, Tim, Greg, et al,
>
> I've tested the following patch on 8.0 and it appears to have the desired
> effect of disabling mtime updates for EA writes and deletes, both
> application-driven and internal to file systems, such as with ACL changes.
> Could I ask you to test it and see if it does the right things in your
> environments?  If so, I'll commit it and get it on the MFC path for 7.2.
>

Hi, Robert.

Tested on 7.1-p2 GENERIC.
Maybe I've missed something, but:

[root@dev2 /tmp]# ls -l ea
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel  10 Jan 26 17:34 ea
                                                         ^^^^^
[root@dev2 /tmp]# setfacl -m g:mail:rw ea
setfacl: acl_get_file() failed: Operation not supported
[root@dev2 /tmp]# setfacl -x g:mail:rw ea
setfacl: acl_get_file() failed: Operation not supported
[root@dev2 /tmp]# setfacl -x g:mail:rw e
setfacl: stat() of e failed: No such file or directory
[root@dev2 /tmp]# getfacl /tmp/ea
# file: /tmp/ea
# owner: root
# group: wheel
user::rw-
group::r--
other::r--
[root@dev2 /tmp]# setfacl -m u::rwx,g:mail:rw /tmp/ea
setfacl: acl_get_file() failed: Operation not supported
[root@dev2 /tmp]# sudo setfacl -m u::rwx,g:mail:rw /tmp/ea
-bash: sudo: command not found
[root@dev2 /tmp]# setfacl -m u::rwx,g:mail:rw /tmp/ea
setfacl: acl_get_file() failed: Operation not supported
[root@dev2 /tmp]# mount -u -o acl /
mount: /dev/aacd0s1a mount option <acl> is unknown: Invalid argument
[root@dev2 /tmp]# mount -u -o acls /
[root@dev2 /tmp]# mount
/dev/aacd0s1a on / (ufs, local, soft-updates, acls)
devfs on /dev (devfs, local)
/dev/aacd0s1e on /var (ufs, local, soft-updates)
/dev/aacd0s1f on /usr (ufs, local, soft-updates)
/dev/aacd0s1g on /home (ufs, local, noatime, soft-updates)
[root@dev2 /tmp]# setfacl -m u::rwx,g:mail:rw /tmp/ea
[root@dev2 /tmp]# ls -l /tmp/ea
-rwxrw-r--+ 1 root  wheel  10 Jan 26 17:45 /tmp/ea
                                                            ^^^^^^

-- 
wbr,
pluknet
